title: fagraea epiphytica
intro: Fagraea epiphytica, also known as the 'Orchid Tree', is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and is characterized by its unique white or yellow fragrant flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. Fagraea epiphytica can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and boosting the immune system.
